sections.label: Introduction
sections.text: A Rest-API framework that will boost the development, Secure the production, and ensures the scalability of your project. Chasi is modeled after an auto-mobile production, which may start with the chassis, and serve's as the car's foundation. v4.1.0 focuses on stability and cluster observability: a rebuilt terminal dashboard with live section tracking, debounced redraws that preserve scroll history, flexible port selection with automatic fallback, and a hardened serviceCluster module with graceful shutdown, correct lead-worker tracking across restarts, terminal-width inheritance for workers, and full compiler compatibility.

requirements.label: Node
requirements.text: Minimum Node.js version required to run chasi-ts.

requirements.label: NPM
requirements.text: Minimum NPM version required to install and manage chasi-ts dependencies.

requirements.label: Git
requirements.text: Minimum Git version required for chasi-ts project management.

glossary.label: Initializing npm
glossary.text: this is the most recommended way of initializing chasi, please note that this step requires node, npm and git installed in your environment.

glossary.label: via chasi-cli
glossary.text: this requires chasi-cli to be globally installed on the system.

glossary.label: Github
glossary.text: via Github fork/clone, etc.

glossary.label: Getting Started
glossary.text: there is a template of environment file right after installation (./template.env), which can be copied as a reference for setting up a .env file.

glossary.label: Configuration
glossary.text: configurations applied to the server

glossary.label: SSR
glossary.text: Chasi's server side rendering is powered by vitejs. checkout ref: https://vitejs.dev/ to learn more.

glossary.label: port
glossary.text: Accepts a single number, an explicit list, or a start/end range object. When the chosen port is already in use the runtime automatically tries the next candidate until one succeeds, then updates process.env.ServerPort and global.__basepath to the resolved value.

glossary.label: port — range
glossary.text: Declare a range as { start: 3010, end: 3020 } in config, or as the string "3010-3020" in the ServerPort env var. The runtime tries each port in order from start to end.

glossary.label: port — list
glossary.text: Declare an explicit list as [3010, 3011, 3012] in config, or as the comma-separated string "3010,3011,3012" in the ServerPort env var. Ports are tried in declaration order.

glossary.label: environment
glossary.text: Server environment to be used, this option will refer to serverConfig.modes[environment] and will launch the configuration set declared from this mode.

glossary.label: cors
glossary.text: this object will be passed to NPM[Cors] package.

glossary.label: serviceCluster
glossary.text: Enables Node.js cluster mode. Chasi forks the configured number of worker processes. Only the lead worker (first forked) forwards structured log sections to the primary's terminal dashboard; all workers forward unhandled exceptions. Set schedulingPolicy to 1 for OS-default or 2 for round-robin (default).

glossary.label: hooks
glossary.text: interact with Chasi's lifecycle

glossary.label: modes
glossary.text: for setting up a specific server environment.

glossary.label: Container
glossary.text: Container configuration is for managing the container registry.

glossary.label: Name
glossary.text: target name for the APP/API/etc.

glossary.label: ControllerDir
glossary.text: Default Controllers Directory, Chasi will autoload this directory as controllers.

glossary.label: ServiceBootstrap
glossary.text: Outside chasi module registry for service providers.

glossary.label: Middlewares
glossary.text: Middlewares must be registered here before it can be applied to any routing container, group, or endpoint.

glossary.label: Logger
glossary.text: A global Logger instance is available throughout the application without any import.

glossary.label: .log()
glossary.text: Outputs plain text to the terminal.

glossary.label: .info()
glossary.text: Outputs an informational message prefixed with ℹ in cyan.

glossary.label: .warn()
glossary.text: Outputs a warning message prefixed with ⚠ in yellow.

glossary.label: .error()
glossary.text: Outputs an error message prefixed with ✖ in red.

glossary.label: Server Start
glossary.text: state[0] server @start lifecycle.

glossary.label: Server Initialize
glossary.text: state[1|2] server @init lifecycle, which initializes DB and routers.

glossary.label: Server boot
glossary.text: state[3] server @boot lifecycle — actual booting of the server.

glossary.label: Terminal Dashboard
glossary.text: Chasi renders a live terminal dashboard on the primary process. It shows a branded header (framework name + version), a runtime status bar (Node version, PID, environment, port, uptime), and per-section blocks for THREADS, PERFORMANCE, DATABASE, ROUTE REGISTRY, SERVICES, BOOT, EXCEPTIONS, LOGS, and WORKERS. The display redraws on every new log entry and on terminal resize, debounced to a single repaint per burst.

glossary.label: Dashboard sections
glossary.text: Each section only renders when it contains at least one entry. Sections render in a fixed order: THREADS → PERFORMANCE → DATABASE → ROUTE REGISTRY → SERVICES → BOOT → EXCEPTIONS → LOGS → WORKERS.

glossary.label: Debounced redraw
glossary.text: Rapid sequences of log writes (e.g. during boot) are collapsed into a single repaint ~50 ms after the last write. The visible screen is erased with \x1b[2J\x1b[H (cursor home) so the scrollback buffer is never cleared — previous terminal output remains accessible by scrolling up.

glossary.label: Resize handling
glossary.text: The dashboard listens to process.stdout resize events and immediately redraws with the new terminal width. In cluster mode, worker processes inherit the primary's terminal width via TERM_COLS so all formatted log entries align correctly.

glossary.label: Service Clustering
glossary.text: Enable Node.js multi-process clustering via serviceCluster in src/config/server.ts. The primary process manages worker lifecycle, pipes structured log data from the lead worker to the dashboard, and handles graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT.

glossary.label: Lead worker
glossary.text: The first forked worker is designated the lead. Only the lead worker forwards structured log sections (DATABASE, BOOT, SERVICES, ROUTE REGISTRY) to the primary's dashboard. All workers forward unhandled exceptions. This prevents N×M duplicate entries when running multiple workers.

glossary.label: Terminal width
glossary.text: Worker processes have no TTY — process.stdout.columns is undefined. The primary injects TERM_COLS into process.env before forking so all workers inherit the correct terminal width. Log formatters (Full, LeftFull, EndTraceFull) use this value to produce correctly-padded output.

glossary.label: Worker restart
glossary.text: When a worker crashes and is re-forked, the replacement inherits the same lead or non-lead role as the process it replaces. The primary tracks leadWorkerId and sets process.env.lead correctly before each re-fork.

glossary.label: Graceful shutdown
glossary.text: On SIGTERM or SIGINT the primary calls worker.disconnect() on each worker, allowing up to 5 seconds for in-flight requests to complete before process.exit(0). Send SIGTERM to the primary PID to trigger a clean shutdown: kill -TERM <pid>.

glossary.label: Storage.destroy()
glossary.text: Call storage.destroy() to remove the terminal resize listener and clear the performance-tracking interval. Prevents listener and timer accumulation when the primary process reinitialises (e.g. hot reload).

glossary.label: Compiler + Cluster
glossary.text: The compiler (Vite SSR) module and serviceCluster can be enabled simultaneously. Workers automatically skip the Vite engine setup — only the primary process initialises the compiler. Set the compiler environment to "prod" for cluster deployments; Vite's dev server (HMR) is not designed for multi-process use.

glossary.label: Cluster error surfacing
glossary.text: Unhandled errors inside the primary's pipe message handler (consumeStream, handleServiceActions, handleSocketActions) are now written to the EXCEPTIONS section of the terminal dashboard instead of being silently dropped.

glossary.label: Router Namespace
glossary.text: A file where routing logic can be applied. The file must export a default function; the route instance is supplied as an argument.

glossary.label: Router Group
glossary.text: Route group is an endpoint wrapper which applies declared options to the wrapped endpoints.

glossary.label: middleware
glossary.text: array of middleware names applied across the group endpoints.

glossary.label: prefix
glossary.text: value will be prepended to the endpoint path.

glossary.label: controller
glossary.text: controller path shorthand for the group.

glossary.label: before
glossary.text: before() event emitted before function/controller execution.

glossary.label: after
glossary.text: after() event emitted before sending a response to the client.

glossary.label: sdk
glossary.text: sdk() handlers applied to every route inside this group. Accepts a single SdkMiddlewareFn or an array. Handlers are prepended before route-level sdk() handlers and are serialised into the generated SDK bundle by sdkBuilder.

glossary.label: Router Endpoint
glossary.text: Routing endpoint points to a controller method or callback function. Endpoints specify the HTTP method and support .middleware(), .sdk(), .except(), and chained options.

glossary.label: RouterServiceProvider
glossary.text: Routers are registered inside the boot method, which is a ServiceProvider method executed before the server @start event.

glossary.label: Router configuration type
glossary.text: router type declaration

glossary.label: name
glossary.text: the value will be used as an index and must be unique.

glossary.label: auth
glossary.text: authentication driver key from config/authentication.ts. Set to false or null to disable auth for the router.

glossary.label: prefix
glossary.text: value appended to all routes registered under this router.

glossary.label: namespace
glossary.text: a string path to a router container file.

glossary.label: middleware
glossary.text: middleware/s applied to all routes in the instance.

glossary.label: controllerdir
glossary.text: a string path/s added to the router's controller path registry.

glossary.label: AuthRouteExceptions?
glossary.text: Routes exempted from auth guards. Exempted routes will not have request.auth populated.

glossary.label: data?
glossary.text: Router hook — declared data is available throughout this Router instance.

glossary.label: mount?
glossary.text: a router hook where third party modules can be registered/mounted.

glossary.label: before?
glossary.text: invoked before a request is passed into the controller/handler function.

glossary.label: after?
glossary.text: invoked before a response is sent back to the client.

glossary.label: displayLog?
glossary.text: option to print route information in the terminal.

glossary.label: sdk?
glossary.text: sdk() handlers applied to every route in this router. Accepts a single SdkMiddlewareFn or an array. Propagated to all endpoints during expansion and serialised by sdkBuilder into the generated bundle. These handlers only run during sdkBuilder compilation — they are cleared from endpoints before the server begins handling requests.

glossary.label: Controller
glossary.text: Handles incoming request and client response.

glossary.label: Database
glossary.text: Chasi Database Handler automatically connects on the declared named connections. Supports MongoDB, Prisma, and Drizzle ORM.

glossary.label: Model
glossary.text: Chasi Model wraps and extends Mongoose Model.

glossary.label: Model Connection
glossary.text: Specify a connection by adding a third argument in Model.connect(). Models default to the default connection if none is specified.

glossary.label: Host
glossary.text: A connection name to connect.

glossary.label: BootWithDB
glossary.text: If enabled, chasi will throw an execution error if one of the DB connections failed.

glossary.label: hideLogConnectionStrings
glossary.text: Hides the connection string in logs.

glossary.label: default
glossary.text: The default connection used by models that don't specify one.

glossary.label: Connections
glossary.text: Named database connection declarations. All entries connect at boot.

glossary.label: Drizzle ORM
glossary.text: Chasi includes a first-class Drizzle ORM driver. Declare a Drizzle connection with driver: "drizzle", then access the live query client via Model.drizzle("connectionName").

glossary.label: adapter
glossary.text: The drizzle-orm sub-path. Supported: "node-postgres", "postgres-js", "mysql2", "better-sqlite3", "libsql".

glossary.label: schema
glossary.text: Path to the schema file relative to src/. Must export Drizzle table definitions as named exports.

glossary.label: globals
glossary.text: Extra options forwarded to the drizzle() constructor.

glossary.label: Querying
glossary.text: Access the Drizzle client via Model.drizzle("connectionName") or this.models.connectionName inside controllers.

glossary.label: Model.drizzle()
glossary.text: Static method on the base Model class that returns the live Drizzle query client for a named connection.

glossary.label: Observer
glossary.text: Chasi's built-in async event system. A single Observer instance is created at boot and exposed as $observer on every Controller.

glossary.label: How events run
glossary.text: Custom events must be listed in src/config/observer.ts before emit() works. The pipeline runs on AsyncEventEmitter, separate from Express request/response timing.

glossary.label: Observer config
glossary.text: Maps custom event aliases to class paths under src/, and defines global beforeEmit and afterEmit hooks.

glossary.label: events
glossary.text: Required registry for custom events. Every alias passed to emit() must appear here.

glossary.label: beforeEmit
glossary.text: Global hook invoked before an event's fire() runs.

glossary.label: afterEmit
glossary.text: Global hook invoked after fire() and when() listeners complete.

glossary.label: ObserverConfig
glossary.text: Type: { events: Record<string,string>; beforeEmit: Function; afterEmit: Function }.

glossary.label: Events
glossary.text: Custom events are classes extending package/Observer/Event.ts and implementing EventInterface.

glossary.label: Event class
glossary.text: Extend Event and implement validate (must call next()) and fire.

glossary.label: $observer.emit()
glossary.text: Dispatch a registered event by alias. Fire-and-forget without await in controllers to return a response while fire() still runs.

glossary.label: $observer.when()
glossary.text: Attach a listener to a registered event.

glossary.label: Lifecycle events
glossary.text: Built-in events: __before__, __initialize__, __after__, __boot__, __ready__, __exception__.

sections.label: Compiler Engine
sections.text: CompilerEngine integrates Vite SSR into Chasi, letting you colocate a Vue, React, or any Vite-compatible frontend alongside your API. Each engine entry maps a Vite project to an Express router prefix and handles dev (HMR) and prod (static build + SSR renderer) modes. Configure it in src/config/compiler.ts.

glossary.label: Compiler Engine
glossary.text: Chasi's built-in Vite SSR integration module. Declared in src/config/compiler.ts and managed by the CompilerEngine class. Engines are set up in parallel during server.hooks.beforeApp on the primary process only.

glossary.label: CompilerEngineConfig
glossary.text: Top-level compiler configuration type. Contains two properties: enabled (master switch) and engines (array of builderConfig entries).

glossary.label: enabled
glossary.text: Master switch. Set to false to disable all Vite compilation — e.g. for API-only deployments. Engines are also automatically skipped when process.env.testMode === "enabled".

glossary.label: engines
glossary.text: Array of engine instances. All engine hooks run in parallel via Promise.all during server.hooks.beforeApp. Each engine must have a unique name and a distinct mountedTo prefix.

glossary.label: builderConfig
glossary.text: Per-engine configuration object. Maps a Vite project root to an Express router prefix and defines how it is built and served in dev and prod modes.

glossary.label: name
glossary.text: Unique engine name. Must match the first prop passed to router.mount() in RouterServiceProvider so Chasi knows which engine to attach to which router.

glossary.label: environment
glossary.text: "dev" starts Vite's dev server with HMR — source files are served directly without a build step. "prod" runs vite.build() for both the SSR server bundle and client bundle, then serves static output.

glossary.label: root
glossary.text: Absolute path to the Vite project directory — the folder containing ssr.config.js and the index.html shell file.

glossary.label: ssrServerModule
glossary.text: Filename of the compiled SSR entry in the .out/server/ output directory. Vite compiles .jsx and .tsx sources to .js, so this must use the output extension — e.g. "entry-server.js" even when the source file is entry-server.jsx.

glossary.label: serverBuild
glossary.text: Vite build options for the Node.js SSR bundle. outDir is relative to root. ssr points to the source entry file (not the output filename). emptyOutDir: true prevents stale build artifacts.

glossary.label: clientBuild
glossary.text: Vite build options for the browser bundle. manifest: true and ssrManifest: true are required — they generate the manifest.json Chasi uses to inject hashed asset URLs into SSR-rendered HTML.

glossary.label: configPath
glossary.text: Absolute path to the Vite config file. A dedicated ssr.config.js is recommended so SSR-specific options (middlewareMode, appType: "custom") stay isolated from any client-only config.

glossary.label: mountedTo
glossary.text: Must exactly match the prefix of the router this engine is attached to in RouterServiceProvider. Chasi passes mountedTo as Vite's base option at build time — it is baked into every asset URL in compiled HTML and JS. A mismatch causes all CSS, JS, and image assets to 404 at runtime.

glossary.label: hook
glossary.text: Called during server.hooks.beforeApp before Express starts accepting requests. In prod mode: Builder.distribute(ctx.root) compiles TS to dist, then Builder.prodSetup(getConfig, ctx) wires static serving and the SSR renderer into Express. Runs on the primary process only.

glossary.label: ssr.config.js
glossary.text: The config file must use the function form: export default defineConfig(() => ({ ... })). Builder.getConfigs() calls the default export as a function — the plain object form defineConfig({}) returns an object and throws TypeError: .default is not a function at boot.

glossary.label: Dev / Prod Modes
glossary.text: Switch modes by changing the environment variable at the top of src/config/compiler.ts. All engines in the array should use the same environment value.

glossary.label: Dev mode
glossary.text: Calls vite.createServer() with middlewareMode: true. HMR is active and files are served directly from source with no build step. Not safe to use when serviceCluster is enabled.

glossary.label: Prod mode
glossary.text: Calls vite.build() for both the SSR server bundle and client bundle, then mounts the compiled output as static files with an SSR renderer. Use prod for CI, staging, and all cluster deployments.

glossary.label: Cluster compatibility
glossary.text: CompilerEngine and serviceCluster can be enabled simultaneously (v4.1.0+). Worker processes skip the Vite engine setup via a cluster.isWorker guard — only the primary process initialises the compiler. Always use environment: "prod" in cluster deployments; Vite's HMR dev server is not designed for multi-process use.

glossary.label: express
glossary.text: Express is a minimal and flexible Node.js web application framework.

glossary.label: ServiceProvider
glossary.text: A way to manage inner/outer modules.

glossary.label: CompilerEngine
glossary.text: chasi internal module that handles SSR and frontend stuff.

glossary.label: HTTP Method
glossary.text: HTTP method of an endpoint, e.g. GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS, PATCH, SEARCH.

glossary.label: Vitest
glossary.text: A native test runner that works with TypeScript. https://vitest.dev/

glossary.label: TDD on Chasi
glossary.text: Test-Driven Development is fully supported by Chasi, powered by vitest.

glossary.label: vitest.config.ts
glossary.text: Vitest can be configured at [<root>/vitest.config.ts]. The compiler engine is automatically skipped when process.env.testMode === "enabled".

glossary.label: test environment
glossary.text: A separate environment file for testing. Set testMode=enabled to suppress compiler engine init and use a dedicated test database.

glossary.label: Testing /users/*
glossary.text: Example test suite for /users endpoints using supertest and vitest.

sections.label: API Spec
sections.text: ApiSpec is a chasi-ts module that auto-generates an OpenAPI 3.x JSON spec file on every server boot. It collects model schemas from all active database connections (MongoDB, Prisma, Drizzle) and route definitions from registered routers, then writes the assembled document to disk.

glossary.label: API Spec
glossary.text: The ApiSpec module initialises during beforeServerBoot() via ApiSpecServiceProvider and writes api.spec.json (or a configured filename) to the project root. Only endpoints that declare a spec option in their route definition appear in the generated document.

glossary.label: Configuration
glossary.text: All ApiSpec options live in src/config/apispec.ts. The file is auto-loaded by Chasi at boot — no registration needed beyond having ApiSpecServiceProvider in ServiceBootstrap.

glossary.label: enabled
glossary.text: Master switch. Set to false to disable spec generation without removing configuration. Can also be toggled via the APISPEC_ENABLED environment variable.

glossary.label: output
glossary.text: Controls where the spec is written. output.filename is resolved from the project root. output.pretty toggles JSON indentation (default true).

glossary.label: definition
glossary.text: Standard OpenAPI document metadata: openapi version string, info (title, version, description), and servers array.

glossary.label: components
glossary.text: Shared component definitions merged into the generated spec. Auto-collected model schemas are added to components.schemas automatically.

glossary.label: security
glossary.text: Global security requirement applied to all protected routes. Routes in AuthRouteExceptions automatically receive security: [] (public).

glossary.label: schemas
glossary.text: Controls which database connections and models are included in the auto-collected components.schemas block.

glossary.label: Schema Collection
glossary.text: Schemas are auto-collected from all active database connections at boot. MongoDB models, Prisma models, and Drizzle tables are all supported. Every generated schema entry includes an x-driver extension field.

glossary.label: keyFormat
glossary.text: Controls how schema keys appear in components.schemas. "plain" uses the bare model name; "prefixed" prepends the connection name (e.g. test:user, pg:properties).

glossary.label: drivers
glossary.text: Connection-level filter. include allowlists specific connection keys; exclude denylists them.

glossary.label: models
glossary.text: Model-level filter keyed by connection name. Applied after the driver filter.

glossary.label: Route Spec
glossary.text: Only endpoints with a spec option appear in the generated document. Tags auto-derive from route.group prefix; auth auto-resolves from AuthRouteExceptions.

glossary.label: spec option
glossary.text: Pass spec as the third argument to any route method. Follows OpenAPI Operation shape.

glossary.label: security
glossary.text: security: [] marks the route as public. Public routes (AuthRouteExceptions) receive security: [] automatically.

glossary.label: tags
glossary.text: If not set, tags auto-derive from the enclosing route.group prefix.

glossary.label: x-middlewares
glossary.text: Effective middlewares written to x-middlewares as an OpenAPI extension on every operation.

glossary.label: Example
glossary.text: route.post('/signup', 'v1/UserController@create', { spec: { summary: 'Register a new user', security: [], requestBody: { required: true, content: { 'application/json': { schema: { type: 'object', required: ['name','email','password'], properties: { name: { type: 'string' }, email: { type: 'string', format: 'email' }, password: { type: 'string', format: 'password' } } } } } }, responses: { 200: { description: 'Created user' }, 400: { description: 'Email already exists' } } } });

sections.label: SDK Builder
sections.text: SdkBuilder is a chasi-ts module that generates a ready-to-use JavaScript SDK bundle from your registered routes. The bundle can be consumed directly by frontend web apps or mobile apps without any additional tooling. It collects route metadata (auth, middlewares, spec) and emits typed request functions grouped by route namespace.

glossary.label: SDK Builder
glossary.text: SdkBuilder initialises during beforeServerBoot() via SdkBuilderServiceProvider. It collects registered routes, runs sdk() validation handlers at build time, generates a JavaScript bundle, and clears all sdk() handlers from endpoints before the server starts accepting requests.

glossary.label: Configuration
glossary.text: All SdkBuilder options live in src/config/sdkbuilder.ts. The file is auto-loaded by Chasi at boot.

glossary.label: enabled
glossary.text: Master switch. Set to false to disable SDK generation.

glossary.label: host
glossary.text: Base URL injected into the generated SDK as HOST.

glossary.label: output
glossary.text: Controls the output filename and formatter. The path is resolved from the project root.

glossary.label: httpClient
glossary.text: HTTP client used in the generated bundle. "fetch" uses the native Fetch API; "axios" requires axios in the consumer app.

glossary.label: routers
glossary.text: Router names to include. Omit to include all registered routers.

glossary.label: exclude
glossary.text: Routes excluded from the generated SDK.

glossary.label: Output & Formatter
glossary.text: The formatter function is called on the raw generated code before writing to disk.

glossary.label: filename
glossary.text: File name or relative path for the generated bundle. Resolved from project root.

glossary.label: formatter
glossary.text: Post-processing function: (code: string) => string | Promise<string>. Omit to write raw output.

glossary.label: terserFormatter
glossary.text: Built-in terser formatter. Import and pass to output.formatter. Falls back to unformatted output if terser fails.

glossary.label: .sdk() Method
glossary.text: The .sdk() method on an Endpoint registers validation handlers that run at build time and are serialised into the generated bundle as inline client-side validators.

glossary.label: .sdk(fn)
glossary.text: Register a single validation handler. Signature: (params, next) => void. Call next() to advance; throw to halt.

glossary.label: .sdk([fn, ...])
glossary.text: Pass an array of handlers — they run in declaration order.

glossary.label: Chaining
glossary.text: Returns the Endpoint instance for chaining: route.post(...).sdk(fn).middleware('auth').

glossary.label: Group-level sdk
glossary.text: Declare sdk on a route.group({ sdk: [fn] }) — prepended to all endpoints in that group.

glossary.label: Router-level sdk
glossary.text: Declare sdk on a RouterConfigInterface — prepended to every route in that router. Execution order: router → group → route.

glossary.label: Generated Bundle
glossary.text: The generated file is an ES module with named exports per route namespace and a default export aggregating all namespaces.

glossary.label: Bundle structure
glossary.text: Each namespace group is exported as a named const. A default export bundles all namespaces.

glossary.label: _validate()
glossary.text: Generated helper that checks required fields from the endpoint's OpenAPI requestBody schema. Throws { status: 422 } on failure.

glossary.label: _request()
glossary.text: Generated helper that makes the HTTP call. Adds Authorization: Bearer <token> for protected routes.

glossary.label: _runSdk()
glossary.text: Generated helper emitted only when at least one route has .sdk() handlers. Executes the serialised handler chain before _request().

glossary.label: Example
glossary.text: Route: route.post('/signin', 'UserController@signin', { spec: { ... } }).sdk((params, next) => { if (!params?.email) throw new Error('email required'); next(); }). Generated: signin: async (payload = {}) => { _validate(payload, ['email','pass']); await _runSdk(payload, [...]); return _request('post', '/api/users/signin', payload); }
